fault_hypothesis: The `overlaps` function at line 76 uses non-strict inequalities (`<=`, `>=`) to check for non-overlapping intervals. This incorrectly classifies adjacent (touching) time slots as non-overlapping, when they should be considered a conflict.

spec.expr: not (a[1] == b[0] or b[1] == a[0]) or __cbfl_result

constraints.intent: If two slots are touching, they must be considered overlapping.
After checking an existing slot, if the loop continues, the candidate must not be touching that slot.

spec.expr: all(candidate[0] != s[1] and candidate[1] != s[0] for s in existing)

constraints.intent: If the candidate is accepted and merged, it must not touch any of the pre-existing slots.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] and any(__cbfl_result[1][i][1] == __cbfl_result[1][i+1][0] for i in range(len(__cbfl_result[1]) - 1)))

constraints.intent: If a candidate is accepted, the resulting schedule must not contain any adjacent (touching) slots.
